meta.title: Personal Custom — streetwave®
meta.description: Order unique customization for sneakers, apparel, or art objects. Tariffs starting at $100.

hero.title: Custom That Expresses You
hero.subtitle: Sneakers, apparel, accessories, and art objects — made to order, designed around your idea, and finished by hand.

tariffs.title: Choose your customization tier

items.name: Malevich
features: 1 art zone (localized area of the surface)
Idea adapted to the shape and material

items.name: Picasso
features: 2–4 art zones or a large central composition
Working with the entire object form

tariffs.disclaimer: Final pricing depends on the complexity, materials, and base item. Design rights remain with the studio. All work is done by hand — minor variations from the sketch are possible.

steps.title: Brief
steps.desc: You describe your idea and share references. We define the scope and timeline.

steps.title: Sketch
steps.desc: We create a detailed visual mockup for your approval.

steps.title: Payment
steps.desc: Once the sketch is approved — 50% upfront payment, and we begin production.

steps.title: Production
steps.desc: Handcrafted by our artists using premium materials.

steps.title: Delivery
steps.desc: Quality control, packaging, and worldwide shipping.

items.q: How long does a custom take?
items.a: 1 to 4 weeks, depending on complexity. Rush projects can be discussed individually.
Sneakers, apparel, accessories, furniture, and more.

items.q: Can I send my own item?
items.a: Yes, as long as the material is suitable for our work.
We require 100% prepayment. After payment, we pass the brief to our designer for mockup development. If the design doesn't suit you, we refund the amount minus the sketch development cost.
After payment and design approval, we hand the project over to our artists.
Yes, during the sketch approval stage.
We provide care instructions for customized items. With proper care, the customization lasts indefinitely.
Yes, we ship worldwide with tracking.

closer.text: Custom isn't decoration. It's a way to express yourself.
